# LinkClean

> LinkClean is a privacy-first iOS app that strips tracking parameters from URLs before you share them — from the share sheet, the app, Shortcuts, or the home-screen widget.

When you share a link from your iPhone, tracking parameters (`utm_source`, `fbclid`, `gclid`, and many more) typically ride along, leaking what you clicked and who sent it. LinkClean removes them — locally, on-device, with no account. The same cleaning happens from the share sheet, from the Clean URL action, from Shortcuts, and from a Control Center / home-screen widget. A scrollable history with link previews makes it easy to grab a previously cleaned link.

## Key facts

- **Name:** LinkClean
- **Product type:** iOS app (native, not a web wrapper)
- **Platform:** iOS 26 or later
- **Pricing:** Free to clean; LinkClean Pro is a one-time in-app purchase that unlocks custom Copy Formats and unlimited custom tracking parameters
- **Audience:** anyone who shares links from iPhone — privacy-minded, designer/PM/dev/journalist/educator workflows
- **Languages:** English, Japanese, German
- **Data:** no account, no sign-up, no email; history is on-device; tracking-cleaning never leaves the device
- **Author / developer:** Ken Tominaga (solo developer) — https://ken0nek.com

## Surfaces (where cleaning happens)

- **Main app** — paste a link, see what was stripped, copy or share the clean version
- **Share extension (Clean URL)** — clean directly from any app's share sheet
- **Shortcuts (App Intents)** — `Clean Link` and `Clean Clipboard` intents you can wire into automations
- **Widget + Control Center** — one-tap "clean the clipboard" from the home screen or Control Center
- **QR scan + generate** — scan a QR code to clean its link, or generate a QR from a cleaned link

## How it differs from a browser extension

Browser extensions only clean what passes through the browser. LinkClean cleans whatever lives in your share sheet or clipboard — links from any app, including native ones the browser can't see.
